title: coreopsis venusta
intro: Coreopsis venusta, also known as the golden ragwort or eastern tickseed, is a perennial wildflower native to the eastern United States. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Coreopsis venusta include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its use as a natural remedy for colds and fever. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for various health purposes.
